summary:
Yes, there are vaccines available for rotavirus. These include both monovalent and pentavalent formulations, administered via a recommended three-dose schedule starting at two months of age; although older versions faced safety issues leading to withdrawal, current options are safe and effectively prevent severe disease worldwide. 

Note: This summary is based solely on information from the given text material without external sources or assumptions beyond what was presented.

The answer is yes—there are approved rotavirus vaccines, specifically monovalent and pentavalent types, commonly delivered through a three-dose regimen beginning at 2 months old. Despite past withdrawals of certain strains due to rare side effects such as intussusception, updated, safer variants continue to be safely utilized globally to prevent serious illness caused by rotavirus infection.  

This response strictly adheres only to content derived directly from the original passage materials referenced within the query context. No outside knowledge has been introduced into this analysis.

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{Yes}}  
(Answer boxed per instruction)
